deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—no rationale about active reasoning, retention, or skill development is offered. The tutor enforces a Socratic approach throughout but never articulates why this supports learning.

In all three conversations, students make meaningful improvements (e.g., correctly identifying geographic unit consistency, choosing appropriate indicators) but the tutor never pauses to prompt reflection on what changed in their thinking or why the improved approach worked. The tutor consistently moves forward without reinforcing method choices after improvement.

grade.overview: The tutor performs strongly across all three transcripts. Socratic method is consistently applied—the tutor never gives direct answers, always prompts the student to reason first, and builds progressively on student responses. Scaffolding is well-executed, with each turn building on prior student contributions. Dialogue quality is excellent: no redundancy, no off-topic drift, and strong assignment anchoring. Communication is concise and appropriately supportive. The two deductions are for (1) never explaining the rationale for the guided approach and (2) missing opportunities to prompt reflection after student improvement.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 is fully intact: the tutor never provides submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently lets students generate key ideas before confirming or redirecting. 1.2 is fully intact: the tutor builds on each student's prior contributions and diagnoses confusion before responding. 1.3 loses 2 points under 1.3.A.a because the tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than answering—no meta-learning rationale is offered in any of the three conversations. 1.3 also loses 2 points under 1.3.C.a because after students make clear improvements (e.g., correctly resolving the geographic unit question, choosing appropriate indicators), the tutor moves forward without prompting reflection on what changed or why the new approach is better. Dialogue and communication quality are both clean across all transcripts.
